title: oenothera boothii subsp. decorticans
intro: Oenothera boothii subsp. decorticans, also known as the White Buffalo Evening Primrose, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Evening Primrose family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Oenothera boothii subsp. decorticans include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to promote wound healing and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by steeping the flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent to extract its medicinal compounds.
